claim: Discovery and exploration benefit from non-determinism (LLMs excel here); credentials, payments, and checkout require determinism for safety
actionable: Separate agent discovery (web search, recommendation) from transaction layers; lock down payment flows to deterministic APIs, not agent-controlled HTML
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Shared payment tokens enable agents to securely share limited-use credentials with sellers; enforce spend limits per currency, amount, time, and seller
actionable: Implement shared payment tokens with scope constraints; agents collect credential from user, create token with limits, share with seller for safe transaction
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: HTTP 402 status code + payment protocol signals to agents they need to pay before accessing a tool; ties payment to specific resource requests
actionable: Return HTTP 402 when agent accesses paid resources; include encoded payload with cost, recipient, payment method; let agent approve and pay
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Agents parse checkout pages poorly; the Agent-Commerce Protocol (ACP) provides structured APIs for products, pricing, shipping, tax instead of web scraping
actionable: Expose product catalogs as JSON APIs (product name, price, images, descriptions); handle checkout state as structured requests/responses; avoid HTML parsing
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Agents are already economic actors spending tokens/credits; expanding payment capabilities to businesses requires safe infrastructure, not just APIs
actionable: Design agent payment systems with same rigor as banking; implement rate limits, spend caps, transaction auditing, and fraud detection
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Sellers need verifiable identity and risk data from agents (brand, last 4 digits) to apply existing fraud systems; transparency is critical
actionable: Share agent identity and payment method metadata with sellers; enable sellers to apply risk models; don't hide agent transactions
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Stripe Projects and recurring budgets leverage shared payment tokens; agents can request repeated access via OAuth-style refresh flows
actionable: Implement recurring payment tokens similar to OAuth flows; agents request subsequent usage within preset budgets; refresh tokens to extend access
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe

claim: Make businesses agent-friendly by exposing APIs, not just web UIs; maximize deterministic flows and minimize agent scraping errors
actionable: Provide API endpoints alongside web interfaces; publish structured product catalogs; enable programmatic checkout; reduce agent brittleness
source_title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe
